title: Oakbridge Systems
preview:
Account snapshot: Oakbridge (mid-tier bank, fintech arm) evaluating Redwood Dedicated for customer-facing conversational assistant + search/rerank pipeline. Heavy focus on deterministic latency and reserved GPU planning. POC running on a small dedicated pool (4xA100 equiv) through Feb-March 2026.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-22: Intro + requirements call (ff-2026-01-22-87421). Captured SLA expectations: p95 < 220ms for single-turn chat (<=1024 tokens), sustained throughput 1200 QPS at peak, burst up to 2k QPS for short windows.
- 2026-01-27: SE deep-dive with Mateo — reviewed model variants, quantization tradeoffs, cold-start scenarios. SE suggested warm pool + pre-warmed KV cache for top-100 prompts.
- 2026-02-05: Security checklist handoff (drive:/Oakbridge/Redwood/SecurityFAQ.pdf). Oakbridge requires SSO (SAML), KMS integration, audit logs retained 3 years, and EU data residency for EU customers.
- 2026-02-18: POC metrics review (ff-2026-02-18-90102). Observed p50 ~85ms, p95 ~260ms on cold nodes during scale-out; after warm-pool tuning p95 dropped to ~190ms for chat path. Reranking pipeline (batching) sustained 800 requests/s with avg latency 120ms.
- 2026-03-02: Legal & procurement initial ask: include clear SLA credits for latency SLO breaches and guaranteed reserved capacity during business hours (MTW-F 06:00-20:00 PST).

Technical requirements & constraints (customer stated):
- Workloads: production chat agent (low latency), reranking for search results (throughput + batching), embeddings for semantic search (bulk jobs nightly).
- Latency SLO targets: p50 < 100ms, p95 < 200ms for chat under committed concurrency; reranker p95 < 300ms when using batching.
- Throughput guarantees: sustained 1200 QPS with 2x burst headroom. Planning for reserved GPU capacity to hit deterministic throughput during market hours.
- Autoscaling expectations: predictable scale-up within 60s for sustained demand increase; scale-down aggressiveness limited (cooldown 10m) to avoid cache loss. Want configurable policies per-route.
- Warm pools: customer requests warm pool sizing guidance. Proposal: maintain warm pool equal to 20% of expected steady-state replicas, with ability to pre-warm additional N replicas 5min before scheduled marketing events.
- Queueing behavior: prefer bounded queue with backpressure and 429 overflow rather than unbounded queue to protect latency SLOs. Need per-customer token-priority routing and soft-priority for interactive sessions.
- Model preferences: open large models (Llama family variants) quantized to int8/4 when acceptable; fallback to smaller family for extreme load.

POC outcomes & learnings:
- Warm pool reduced p95 by ~25% in the chat path during sudden bursts.
- Continuous batching in rerank path increased throughput but introduced tail latency spikes when batch sizes grew; solution: adaptive max-batch-size by latency budget.
- Cold-start cost was non-trivial: observed 1.8x CPU/GPU utilization spike during first 2 minutes of scale-up for new replicas due to KV cache warm-up.

Open negotiation items (for Redwood sales/SE):
- SLA appendix wording: define what constitutes an outage vs latency SLO breach, credit calculation, measurement windows. Oakbridge wants business-hour capacity guarantees for key routes.
- Warm-pool pricing model: include pre-warm cost in reserved capacity quote or as separate line item. Customer leans toward single monthly commit if warm pool pricing included.
- Autoscaler tuning defaults: provide recommended scale-up latency targets, cooldowns, and diagnostic dashboards exposed to customer.
- Fallback policy: automatic route to smaller model variant when top-tier pool saturated; need agreement on quality/regression monitoring.

Action items / next steps:
- (SE) Mateo to deliver warm-pool sizing worksheet and cost model by 2026-03-16 (drive:/Oakbridge/Redwood/POC-metrics.xlsx).
- (AE) Leah to circulate draft SLA appendix (including latency credit calc) to Oakbridge legal by 2026-03-18.
- (Customer) Oakbridge infra team to provide expected hourly traffic curve for next 6 months to finalize reserved GPU planning.
- (All) Schedule procurement + technical sign-off meeting week of 2026-03-22.

Quotes & snippets from stakeholders:
- "We need predictable p95 latency during market hours — variance kills UX." — Head of Product, Oakbridge.
- "If pre-warming is going to cost extra, we need a clean comparison: committed vs on-demand + warm-pool line item." — Infra procurement lead.

Risk notes:
- If scale-down is too aggressive we risk cache thrash and elevated latency at peaks. Keep conservative cooldowns.
- Legal needs explicit measurement methodology in SLA (which logs/region counts). SE to recommend telemetry/instrumentation endpoints.

Recommended Redwood positioning for close:
- Offer a reserved pool + optional prioritized warm pool (20% baseline) with explicit p95 latency SLO for business hours, and a credit scheme for misses.
- Provide a one-month pilot at reduced pricing to finalize autoscaler tuning and warm-pool sizing under real traffic.

CRM shorthand (for internal use):
- Stage: evaluation -> close target 2026-04. POC succeeded but legal/SLA + pricing are blockers. High ARR potential if committed warm pool included.
- Next internal check: Leah + Mateo sync 2026-03-15 to finalize worksheet.

title: Roseglen ProductCloud
preview: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics platform) evaluating Redwood Hosted API as fast path, then Dedicated reserved capacity for EU data residency and predictable throughput. Focus on EU-primary deployment with multi-region failover, cost/latency tradeoffs and legal data residency language. POC scoped to chat support assistant + embeddings for search.
text: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics platform) evaluating Redwood Hosted API as fast path, then Dedicated reserved capacity for EU data residency and predictable throughput. Focus on EU-primary deployment with multi-region failover, cost/latency tradeoffs and legal data residency language. POC scoped to chat support assistant + embeddings for search.

title: Echo Lattice Software
preview:
Snapshot: mid-market product analytics SaaS. Primary goal: add conversational assistant + in-app doc search that is production-grade and privacy-safe.

Sales / technical story: started with Hosted API to iterate on prompts and embedding quality; POC validated prompt templates, embedding vectors, and latency profiles. Product team now wants Dedicated in a VPC for data isolation and compliance.

Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-02-10: Intro demo (Jordan, Priya) — walked through hosted flows, streaming, and embedding previews. Fireflies ff-2457.
- 2026-02-20: POC kick-off — mirror 2 product flows (support-chat, doc-search) on hosted API; Redwood provided sample batching/quant config.
- 2026-02-28: Mid-POC checkpoint call — reported p95 latency ~220ms on hosted for chat (seq len 256), embeddings quality pass for retrieval.
- 2026-03-05: Security intake opened — Echo Lattice security team requested SOC2 artifacts, SAML integration patterns, audit log schema, retention controls, and network egress list. Jira RDW-4821 created for SAML questions.
- 2026-03-09: Pricing sync — AE presented Dedicated pilot rates and estimated ARR impact. Revenue owner requested a 6-month reserved capacity option for predictable unit economics.
- 2026-03-10: Security working session scheduled — scope: KMS integration, SSO mapping, and data residency confirmation.

Requirements summary (shorthand):
- Workloads: chat (priority), embeddings (semantic search), summarization (doc pipelines).
- Latency / throughput targets: target p50 ~50-80ms for short chat; p95 budget <=250ms; peak concurrent qps target ~200-400 for scaled beta.
- Cost sensitivity: mid (willing to commit for discount but needs clear unit economics).
- Model preferences: small/med open models for embeddings; prefer ability to pin model version and automated fallback to cheaper variant when capacity constrained.
- Routing/fallback: must support region routing (NA primary) and fallback to lower-cost variant under load.
- Security: SOC2 compliance required, SSO/SAML (Okta), audit logs with day-level retention and export, KMS for envelope encryption, explicit retention controls (30/90/365 options).

Quotes / call notes:
- CTO: "we can't have any unredacted PII leave our VPC — need strong KMS and tokenization options."
- VP Product: "If Dedicated VPC pricing looks reasonable we'll aim GA for Q3; Hosted is great for experiments but not for production."
- SE feedback (Priya): prompt caching + batching suggested to hit unit cost targets; propose Redwood Optimize configs.

Open items / action list:
- Provide Dedicated VPC architecture diagram (network, ingress/egress, peering options).
- Send redacted SOC2 report + controls matrix and sign DPA.
- Confirm SAML attribute mapping example for Okta IdP (Echo Lattice security to share metadata).
- Provide static egress IP list or NAT guidance for their firewall rules.
- Finalize pilot pricing and SLA for reserved capacity.

Stubs / refs: pricing deck -> drive:/redwood/presentations/echo-lattice-poc-pricing.pdf; SOC2 summary -> drive:/security/soc2-packages/2025-redwood-soc2-summary.pdf; Jira -> RDW-4821; Fireflies -> ff-2457-echo-lattice-demo.

Short-term next: VPC diagram + SOC2 artifacts drop this week; schedule 60-min security review with Echo Lattice infra and legal.

CRM tone: fragmented notes, action-driven, ping legal & infra teams.

title: Veritas Pulse Platforms
preview: Platform runs user-facing support chat (SLA: 99th pct < 250ms for interactive sessions), embedding-based search across 80M documents for compliance, and a reranking service for fraud alerts. Current hosted setup is stable but cost per token and tail-latency during morning peak are above target. Wants Dedicated upgrade and Optimize to hit cost/latency goals; requires EU-region residency for EEA customers and strict audit logging for regulated lines of business.
text: Platform runs user-facing support chat (SLA: 99th pct < 250ms for interactive sessions), embedding-based search across 80M documents for compliance, and a reranking service for fraud alerts. Current hosted setup is stable but cost per token and tail-latency during morning peak are above target. Wants Dedicated upgrade and Optimize to hit cost/latency goals; requires EU-region residency for EEA customers and strict audit logging for regulated lines of business.

title: WardenBridge Archive Solutions
preview: High-security gov customer. Procurement-heavy, long procurement windows. Wants on-prem air-gapped deployment or private VPC with HSM and strict retention controls. POC scoped to 3 months. AE has relationship via prior org. Quote from CISO: "cannot accept exfil risk; need full offline audit trail and FIPS modules". SE feedback: heavy emphasis on reranker reproducibility and deterministic embeddings across quantized variants. Likes Redwood runtime's kernel selection idea but needs IL5 proof points. Drive links above: security FAQ + HW spec. Short-form: POC -> security review -> SOW -> procurement (GFE servers).

title: SparkKnock Play
preview: SMB mobile/social games. Hosted API inbound. Needs low-latency chat + on-request moderation. Discovery stage; AE Laura assigned.

title: Cerium Vertex Infrastructure
preview: Private VPC-only deployment with no public egress for sensitive workloads,BYOK option + HSM support (customer-managed CMK) with key rotation policy,Audit logs: immutable, exportable, retention 7 years, access controls and SIEM integration (Splunk/SumoLogic),SIG/CAIQ completion required; gap remediation plan for items failing vendor intake,Shared responsibility matrix showing which telemetry and backups Redwood retains vs customer-owned storage,Regional data residency: keep training data and embeddings in region (EU for EU accounts) — ability to tag and restrict models per region,Network: support for VPC peering / PrivateLink / customer-managed NAT gateways; explicit IP allowlist for admin consoles,Operational SLOs: p95 latency targets per workload, canary rollouts for model version changes, incident escalation contact list

title: Guardian LogWorks, Inc.
preview:
Summary: Guardian LogWorks is evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to replace an aging in-house inference pipeline for customer-facing fraud-detection and support triage. Primary decision levers are auditability and data residency. InfoSec is focused on SIEM integration (Splunk/QRadar), log schema parity with existing telemetry, and BYOK/KMS with HSM attestation.

Key requirements / asks (verbatim & paraphrase):
- "Customer quote" : "We need a 1:1 mapping of inference events into our SIEM with the same fields as app logs (request_id, user_id, model_version, prompt_hash, tokens_in, tokens_out, decision, latency_ms, route, node_id)".
- SIEM export: prefer syslog-over-TLS (CEF/LEEF mapping) and HTTPS push for cloud SIEMs; expect retention to be handled in SIEM but require ability to export raw logs for eDiscovery.
- Log schema: must include stable request_id (traceable to upstream app), timestamp in ISO8601 UTC, tenant_id/customer_id, model metadata, token counts, latency breakdown (client->edge, edge->runtime, runtime->model), and a cryptographic hash of the prompt (for PII minimization).
- Access logs: admin API usage, token generation events, and key-access events must be separately exportable and time-window searchable.
- Retention: Legal requests a minimum 7 years for audit logs for certain regulated customers; InfoSec will accept 1 year in platform if automatic archival/export to customer-managed S3 with lifecycle rules is implemented.
- KMS/BYOK: must support customer-managed keys (AWS KMS, GCP KMS) and HSM-backed key material. Key rotation policy and proof of non-repudiation required.
- Data residency: production inference for EU customers must run in eu-central region; logs for EU tenants must not leave EU without documented consent.
- Network: VPC peering or PrivateLink required; strict egress rules, minimal public endpoints; support for private endpoint for SIEM ingest or ability to stage logs to customer S3 bucket.

POC scope asked by customer:
- Provide example audit log stream (15k events) mapped to customer's Splunk CEF spec
- Provide config for syslog-over-TLS forwarder + HTTPS webhook to SIEM (sample nginx + vector config)
- Demonstrate BYOK integration with sample key and rotation notes
- Show retention/export flow to customer-managed S3 + lifecycle rule example

Pending items / risks:
- Legal needs to sign off on 7-year retention approach; product currently offers 1 year by default in-host with archival available via export.
- Customer requests log schema including prompt_hash; InfoSec flagged possible PII leakage — needs guidance from Product on prompt hashing semantics and deterministic vs salted hashing.
- Timeline: customer has internal procurement window end of May 2026; engineering budget signoff needed to finalize Dedicated vs Private pricing.

References / stubs: security deck in Drive, sample log schema PDF, Jira INFRA-4287 about syslog connector, Fireflies call records.

Short next steps (AE notes):
- 1) SE to deliver sample log export artifact + Splunk mapping by 2026-03-15.
- 2) Schedule legal + security sync w/ customer 2026-03-18.
- 3) Prep BYOK runbook and HSM attestation example for customer review.

CRM shorthand: "need sample logs + syslog config. legal retention hold. BYOK attestation requested. procurement target May. SE on it."

title: Sable Orbit Technologies
preview:
Summary: Enterprise procurement for Private VPC deployment. Heavy security focus (data residency EU option, KMS BYOK/HSM, audit log forwarding to customer SIEM).

Recent timeline:
- 2025-11-20: Initial intro call (Jordan). basic use-case: cockpit analytics + pilot support chat. asked about private deployment options.
- 2025-12-08: Demo of hosted API. positive on latency. asked for private VPC pricing.
- 2026-01-15: Architecture deep-dive with infra (Priya). discussed VPC endpoints, peering vs PrivateLink, egress control.
- 2026-02-18: Security intake call (Fireflies id above). procurement/infosec requested SIG/CAIQ and evidence for SOC2 controls.
- 2026-02-25: Uploaded preliminary SIG/CAIQ to procurement portal. Legal flagged gaps on key rotation & BYOK.
- 2026-03-05: Network review meeting; need CIDR, route table examples, NAT/bastion details.

Security questionnaire status:
- SIG/CAIQ: submitted (partial). security team requested encrypted evidence (pdfs) for 20+ control families.
- Vendor risk intake: Procurement opened intake ticket JIRA-INF-2110. assigned risk owner Sarah G.
- Shared responsibility: customer expects delineation for key management, audit retention, and VPC network controls. They want a formal matrix before final contract.

Technical requirements extracted:
- VPC-only private endpoints (no public egress by default).
- KMS integration: BYOK preferred; HSM-backed keys for model cache & logs. Key rotation policy required.
- Audit logs: full request/response metadata retained for 180 days in EU region; forward to customer SIEM (syslog/HTTP webhook).
- Data residency: primary region EU-West. fallbacks to NA only with prior approval.
- Authentication: SSO (SAML) + SCIM provisioning for RBAC.
- Compliance: Must provide SOC2 Type II report and ISO27001 certificate; pen-test summary for last 12 months.

POC & next milestones:
- Week of 2026-03-15: Security workshop with Sable Orbit infra + Redwood security (Priya + Mateo) — goal: finalize shared-resp matrix and KMS runbook.
- 2026-03-22: Deliver updated CAIQ with evidence zip and SOC2 attestation.
- 2026-03-29: Network diagram + example Terraform snippets for VPC peering and private endpoints.
- April 2026: Procurement review + legal SOW negotiation (target).

Quotes / customer color:
- "We can't have logs leave EU without explicit approval — that is a hard stop." — Sable Orbit InfoSec
- "Need BYOK, otherwise our security team will not sign." — Procurement lead

Open questions / items to own for Redwood:
- Provide documented shared-responsibility matrix (template from Security + annotated).
- KMS BYOK flow: key import vs HSM-backed rotation options; sample API callflow for key wraps.
- Audit log retention SLA and export capability (how to push to customer SIEM in near real-time).
- Network runbook: required ports, egress controls, example NAT configuration.

Action items for AE/SE/CSM:
- Jordan: coordinate procurement timeline and escalate SOC2 evidence to trust center.
- Priya (SE): prepare VPC Terraform snippets, KMS integration workshop slides.
- Mateo (CSM): draft onboarding checklist + 90-day rollout plan with canary routes.

Attachments: pricing deck (drive link), preliminary SIG/CAIQ export (drive link), Fireflies transcript link.

Short form next steps / blockers (CRM style):
- Next: Security workshop — confirm attendees + shared-resp matrix.
- Blocker: Legal needs full CAIQ + evidence zipped; procurement hold until SOC2 attestation verified.

CRM notes tone: lots of fragments, shorthand from calls, repeated asks from InfoSec. Keep tracking on procurement portal.
text:
Summary: Enterprise procurement for Private VPC deployment. Heavy security focus (data residency EU option, KMS BYOK/HSM, audit log forwarding to customer SIEM).

Recent timeline:
- 2025-11-20: Initial intro call (Jordan). basic use-case: cockpit analytics + pilot support chat. asked about private deployment options.
- 2025-12-08: Demo of hosted API. positive on latency. asked for private VPC pricing.
- 2026-01-15: Architecture deep-dive with infra (Priya). discussed VPC endpoints, peering vs PrivateLink, egress control.
- 2026-02-18: Security intake call (Fireflies id above). procurement/infosec requested SIG/CAIQ and evidence for SOC2 controls.
- 2026-02-25: Uploaded preliminary SIG/CAIQ to procurement portal. Legal flagged gaps on key rotation & BYOK.
- 2026-03-05: Network review meeting; need CIDR, route table examples, NAT/bastion details.

Security questionnaire status:
- SIG/CAIQ: submitted (partial). security team requested encrypted evidence (pdfs) for 20+ control families.
- Vendor risk intake: Procurement opened intake ticket JIRA-INF-2110. assigned risk owner Sarah G.
- Shared responsibility: customer expects delineation for key management, audit retention, and VPC network controls. They want a formal matrix before final contract.

Technical requirements extracted:
- VPC-only private endpoints (no public egress by default).
- KMS integration: BYOK preferred; HSM-backed keys for model cache & logs. Key rotation policy required.
- Audit logs: full request/response metadata retained for 180 days in EU region; forward to customer SIEM (syslog/HTTP webhook).
- Data residency: primary region EU-West. fallbacks to NA only with prior approval.
- Authentication: SSO (SAML) + SCIM provisioning for RBAC.
- Compliance: Must provide SOC2 Type II report and ISO27001 certificate; pen-test summary for last 12 months.

POC & next milestones:
- Week of 2026-03-15: Security workshop with Sable Orbit infra + Redwood security (Priya + Mateo) — goal: finalize shared-resp matrix and KMS runbook.
- 2026-03-22: Deliver updated CAIQ with evidence zip and SOC2 attestation.
- 2026-03-29: Network diagram + example Terraform snippets for VPC peering and private endpoints.
- April 2026: Procurement review + legal SOW negotiation (target).

Quotes / customer color:
- "We can't have logs leave EU without explicit approval — that is a hard stop." — Sable Orbit InfoSec
- "Need BYOK, otherwise our security team will not sign." — Procurement lead

Open questions / items to own for Redwood:
- Provide documented shared-responsibility matrix (template from Security + annotated).
- KMS BYOK flow: key import vs HSM-backed rotation options; sample API callflow for key wraps.
- Audit log retention SLA and export capability (how to push to customer SIEM in near real-time).
- Network runbook: required ports, egress controls, example NAT configuration.

Action items for AE/SE/CSM:
- Jordan: coordinate procurement timeline and escalate SOC2 evidence to trust center.
- Priya (SE): prepare VPC Terraform snippets, KMS integration workshop slides.
- Mateo (CSM): draft onboarding checklist + 90-day rollout plan with canary routes.

Attachments: pricing deck (drive link), preliminary SIG/CAIQ export (drive link), Fireflies transcript link.

Short form next steps / blockers (CRM style):
- Next: Security workshop — confirm attendees + shared-resp matrix.
- Blocker: Legal needs full CAIQ + evidence zipped; procurement hold until SOC2 attestation verified.

CRM notes tone: lots of fragments, shorthand from calls, repeated asks from InfoSec. Keep tracking on procurement portal.

title: Clinicentrix Health Search
preview:
Summary: Clinicentrix is building an enterprise-grade clinical document search used by 200+ hospital clinicians. Primary focus: large-scale embeddings + cost-aware reranking pipeline for PHI-protected EHR notes.

Recent activity:
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Ava) — high-level product fit; they indexed a 30M note prototype.
- 2026-01-22: Deep-dive with SE (Mateo) — architecture constraints, ingestion cadence, delta indexing. Fireflies: ff:call_987654321.
- 2026-02-18: Security sync with InfoSec (Rita joined) — SSO, KMS, audit retention discussed; asked for SOC2 evidence and network topology.
- 2026-03-03: Cost model review; asked for POC SOW and expected token/embedding pricing for 120M doc index; we sent initial estimate (drive:/Sales/Deals/Clinicentrix/2026/Clinicentrix_Redwood_POC_Deck_v3.pdf).

Technical requirements / asks (customer language fragments):
- "Need P95 search latency <200ms for single-clinic queries; multi-site aggregate queries can be higher."
- Index size target: ~120M clinical notes (de-identified + PHI flagged for access controls).
- Throughput goal: sustained 150 QPS peak for search surface, burst to 500 QPS during morning rounds.
- Approach preferred: two-stage retrieval — sparse/pooled doc filter -> bi-encoder embeddings -> lightweight re-rank on top-50 -> cross-encoder rerank only for top-5 when allowed. Cost sensitivity: cross-encoder rerank on-host preferred to avoid hosted per-token costs.
- Embedding model: prefer open/traceable model; will evaluate Redwood-verified candidate models. Must measure semantic drift vs. clinical queries.
- Ingest: near real-time EHR stream, dedupe, redaction pipeline before indexing; want connectors run in VPC or on-prem agent.

Security / compliance specifics:
- PHI: must not leave customer-controlled enclave for protected fields; they want split-processing pattern (embeddings hashed in VPC, identifiable PHI stored in encrypted columns).
- Audit: full access logs shipped to their SIEM, 1y retention for audit trails + tamper-evident hashes.
- Key management: HSM/KMS integration mandatory for production.
- Data residency: US for majority, but pilot includes EU hospital (data residency question).

POC success criteria:
- Index 5M clinical notes into a private Redwood Dedicated pool in VPC with P95 <200ms at 150 QPS using bi-encoder + top-50 rerank.
- Demonstrate audit logging (immutable events exported) and KMS-encrypted keys for models.
- Cost projection for 120M index with expected 2.5M queries/month and 20% cross-encoder rerank traffic.

Open questions (sales/tech):
- Which Redwood model variants can be hosted in Dedicated with HSM-backed keys?
- Can automatic fallback be enforced to private model variant when capacity in hosted tier is low?
- Exact SLA for multi-region failover (we need an architecture doc).

Stated blockers / risks:
- Legal needs explicit PHI data flow diagram before POC kickoff.
- Networking team requires a 3-week maintenance window to enable VPC peering.
- Cost sensitivity: customer requests a discounted pilot rate and strict per-query budget cap.

Next steps (short):
- Book architecture + security workshop (internal invite: Mateo, Rita, Ava + Clinicentrix infra and InfoSec) — target week of 2026-03-15.
- Deliver POC SOW + line-item cost model by 2026-03-12 (action: Ava).
- Provide network topology template + sample data-flow diagram for PHI handling (action: Mateo).

title: Northpeak AI
preview:
Account summary (Northpeak AI)
- Strong tail-latency focus; they evaluate vendors primarily on p95/p99 under mixed prompt lengths.
- Quote from deep dive: "Our short chat requests can’t get stuck behind long-context jobs. Users notice immediately." They described classic head-of-line blocking symptoms.

Workload + requirements
- Primary: chat generation for support agent + internal ops assistant.
- Secondary: summarization + doc search (embeddings), but their gating factor is chat tail latency.
- Traffic pattern: mix of short interactive prompts (typical chat) + periodic long-context prompts (importing large ticket threads / knowledge packs). Long-context can spike during batch workflows.
- Success metrics (as stated by them / aligned in call):
  - Improve or hold p95 TTFT for short prompts during long-context spikes.
  - Keep throughput roughly flat (they’ll tolerate small hit if p95 improves materially).
  - Fairness/progress guarantee for long prompts (no starvation) + clear guardrails.

Deployment + security
- Prefers Dedicated in VPC (data isolation + consistent latency). Not asking for on-prem.
- Needs SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration, retention controls; asked about regional pinning (US-only initially, EU later).
- They requested clarity on what telemetry we store (token-level analytics vs metadata-only options).

Mixed-workload pain / Redwood angle
- Their current setup sees p95 tail amplification when long-prefill or long-context requests get mixed into the same queue/batch selection.
- We positioned the mixed-workload scheduler prototype (short/long classification + two-queue dispatch + aging/starvation prevention + per-class token budgets) as the direct mitigation.
- They specifically asked for visibility into: queue depth by class, time-in-queue, per-class p95, and starvation counters during canary/POC.

Artifacts referenced
- Fireflies deep dive transcript: ff-2025-11-19-technical-deep-dive-tail-latency-and-mixed-workloads.
- Internal canary approval thread referenced in email: 20251203-approval-request-production-canary-mixed-workload-scheduler.
- Benchmark raw runs + canary plan drafts linked in Drive (see linked_drive_docs).

Open questions (to answer in benchmark readout)
- Confirm their exact prompt-length distribution + long-context ceiling (tokens) and whether they can share anonymized stats.
- Target models: they mentioned "open model preferred" but did not commit; need to pick 1-2 candidates for apples-to-apples.
- Clarify SLO definition: do they measure end-to-end (client) or server-side; and whether they care more about TTFT vs total generation time.

Next steps (proposed)
1) Have Claire Dubois run benchmark-lab mixed scenarios (80/20 short/long + long-spike) using their provided distributions; produce a 1-page results summary (p95 TTFT, p95 latency, throughput deltas).
2) Share recommended scheduler defaults (classification threshold + aging parameters + per-class token budgets) and how rollback works.
3) Schedule a security architecture review (VPC + KMS + audit logs + retention) with Northpeak security lead.
4) If benchmarks clear, move to procurement checklist + paper SLO/terms draft.

title: Northstar Privacy AI
preview: Primary workload: high-sensitivity document search + redaction in customer support and investigations. Must process PII and regulated financial docs. Expect mostly embedding + reranking pipeline with occasional chat assistants. Latency SLOs: 150-300ms p50 generation for short queries; throughput: sustained 200 QPS for embeddings during peak batch jobs. Cost sensitivity moderate; willingness to trade cost for strict residency and auditability.

Expect mostly embedding + reranking pipeline with occasional chat assistants. Latency SLOs: 150-300ms p50 generation for short queries; throughput: sustained 200 QPS for embeddings during peak batch jobs. Cost sensitivity moderate; willingness to trade cost for strict residency and auditability.

title: Sterling Citadel Systems
preview:
Topline: large defense-tier contact center integrator. Onboarding for mission-critical 24/7 ops. Must be fully air-gapped for classification boundary; cannot allow outbound tokens.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: live agent assist (real-time cues), post-call ticket summarization (structured), tool-calling to case mgmt and escalation workflows, and automated surge handling during incidents.
- Perf targets: median agent assist latency <120ms for token stream (per-agent), sustained throughput 10k tokens/sec during peak events, burst to 50k tokens/sec for incident windows.
- Reliability: automatic failover to local fallback models when primary unavailable; prefer deterministic degrade to text-only (no remote fallthrough).
- Models: open-model preference for on-prem licensing; need support for quantized LLMs and ability to pin to specific model versions.
- Security: FIPS-validated crypto, HSM integration for key storage, STIG-hardened images, full audit logging with 7+ years retention for certain records, role-based redaction pipeline for PII.

Recent meeting notes (shorthand):
- 2026-01-22: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_2026-01-22_sterling-arch-review). AE Jordan + SE Priya. They sketched air-gap architecture. Customer: "no egress, no exceptions".
- 2026-01-29: Deep-dive with infra/security team. Provided security FAQ (drive link). Customer asked for STIG checklist and vendor attestation sample.
- 2026-02-03: Proof-of-concept scoping call. Agreed POC to run in their SOC2 lab for 3 weeks, STIGed VM images, and local-only model bundle. SE to deliver POC plan by 2026-02-10.
- 2026-02-05: Pricing/contract framing; procurement asked for 3 customer references with on-prem CCaaS integration.

Tech considerations / tradeoffs called out:
- Tool-calling: need secure connectors to Genesys/Avaya on-prem APIs; connector must be packaged as appliance - no outbound webhook to Redwood cloud.
- KV/prefix caching must be local-only; any caching telemetry must be scrubbed or kept offline.
- Observability: they want token-level cost breakdown but only via on-prem console; cannot ship raw traces offsite.
- Quantization: recommend 8-bit LLAMA variants; customer wants Perplexity/QA results run on their eval suite.

POC acceptance criteria (customer-specified):
- On-prem bundle deployed into isolated lab with no internet.
- Real-time assist latency under 180ms 95th pct during 1k concurrent agents.
- Tool-calling workflows executed end-to-end with audit logs captured locally.
- Ability to roll back model version with <5 minute downtime.

Next steps (short):
- SE (Priya) to deliver detailed POC runbook and STIG checklist by 2026-02-10.
- Legal to provide draft MSA with indemnities for air-gapped deployment.
- Customer to supply procurement RFQ and three reference contacts.

Quoted lines/quotes: "If you can't run inside our enclave, it won't pass procurement." - Director of Support.

CRM extras: Gmail thread gm_thread_Ab12cdEfGhIJ has pricing discussion; JIRA-CC-4021 opened for connector requirements.

Short-term risks: procurement timeline (Q2 2026), FedRAMP expectation may push timeline out, reference requirement could delay award.

Contact cadence: weekly on Wed 10am PT until POC starts.

title: Mosaic Archive Systems
preview:
Summary/CRM notes:
- Company: Mosaic Archive Systems, specializes in indexing decades of media: transcripts, closed captions, scripts, editorial metadata, and OCR from tape transfers. Content sizes are long: single assets 5k-250k tokens.
- Primary problem: search performance for journalist and legal teams. Need high recall + top-k precision. Currently using Elastic + nightly pipeline; want denser recall and reranking to cut manual curation.
- Scale: expect 20-60M chunks after fine-grained chunking (scene-level + sliding windows). Index growth steady 5-8% mo.
- Latency targets: 95th <= 300ms for search-retrieval+rerank in dedicated/private deployment for interactive UX. For legal workflows can accept 1-2s for deep rerank.
- Cost target: aiming for ~$0.01-0.05 per interactive query end-to-end (embedding lookup + reranker compute amortized). This drives conversation about quantized models, batching, and caching.
- Chunking strategy notes from call: prefer hierarchical chunking: (1) coarse segmentation by asset/scene markers, (2) variable-length chunks capped at 4k tokens, (3) overlapping edges 256-512 tokens for boundary context, (4) store original offsets and shot timestamps as metadata. Also exploring keyframe-extracted captions to create small dense anchors for fast re-ranking.
- Retrieval architecture discussed: hybrid sparse+dense at query time. Use TF-IDF to prune to 1000 candidates, then bi-encoder to 200, then cross-encoder reranker top 20. SE recommends moving to learned reranker with hard-negative mining from user clicks.
- Reranker tuning: initial cross-encoder fine-tune on curated 5k labeled pairs (relevance judgments from editorial team). Hard-negative mining schedule: sample top model negatives + human-curated non-relevants; fine-tune 3 epochs with warmup. Aim to distill cross-encoder into a lightweight late-interaction model for sub-300ms p95.
- Ops notes: prefix caching for recent queries, persistent KV cache for long narratives, and shard-aware routing to keep asset-locality for low-latency. Suggest Redwood Optimize: recommendations on batching windows and quantization profile (int8 for bi-encoder, int4 for generator/reranker distill variant).
- Security/compliance: must support on-prem install, KMS integration, audit logs for all query retrievals, SSO (SAML). Data residency: keep EU and NA assets separate.
- Quote from head of product: 'We need accuracy first but will kill spend if latency spikes. If reranker beats baseline, we'll convert current Elastic spend into Redwood dedicated contract.'
- Next actions: finalize POC dataset export (engineering), Redwood to supply dedicated cluster sizing and cost model, legal to confirm tape ingestion terms. Blockers: ingress timing, final ARR sign-off, reranker latency tradeoffs.
- Quick notes: watch tokenization differences on OCRed text, aggressive normalization hurts reranker. Recommend normalizing dates/names but keeping verbatim sequences for legal queries.
